steps.title: Test the proxy idea
steps.content: The hypothesis is that CeO2-NP bioaccumulation in zebra mussels could serve as a proxy for bioaccumulation testing in rainbow trout. For that to work, mussel uptake would need to reliably indicate trout uptake.

steps.title: Reject nonweakening findings
steps.content: Choice A supports reliability by showing little individual variation in both species. Choice C compares other nanoparticle types, not whether CeO2-NP uptake matches between the two species. Choice D gives a possible advantage of mussels but does not weaken proxy validity.

steps.title: Break the proxy link
steps.content: The remaining finding weakens the hypothesis because unexplained differences in CeO2-NP uptake rates would make mussels unreliable substitutes for trout. The answer is B.
